title: USDA FoodData Central
notes: All foods — calories, fat, sat_fat, protein, fiber: Primary source for all nutritional values, reported per gram.

title: MyFoodData — Almonds Nutrition Facts
notes: Almonds — nutrition verification: Confirmed caloric and macronutrient values against USDA FoodData Central.

title: Recipal — Ground Beef Nutrition Facts
notes: Beef — nutrition verification: Confirmed nutritional values for 80% lean raw ground beef against USDA data.

title: FoodStruct — Wheat Nutrition Facts
notes: Wheat — nutrition verification: Confirmed whole grain wheat nutritional values against USDA data.

title: FoodStruct — Soybean Nutrition Facts
notes: Soy — nutrition verification: Confirmed cooked soybean nutritional values against USDA data.

title: FatSecret — Chicken Breast Nutrition Facts (USDA)
notes: Chicken — nutrition verification: Confirmed cooked chicken breast nutritional values against USDA data.

title: ScienceDirect — Almond Water Footprint Study
notes: Almonds — water_per_kg: Reports 10,240 L/kg average for California commercial orchards.
Almonds — water_per_kg averaging: Averaged with Mekonnen & Hoekstra (source 30) value of 13,080 L/kg to produce final value of 11,660 L/kg.

title: Our World in Data — Almond Yields
notes: Almonds — yield_kg_ha: Verified ~3,000 kg/ha for California commercial orchards.

title: Our World in Data — Wheat Yields
notes: Wheat — yield_kg_ha: Source for global average of 2,067 kg/ha.

title: Our World in Data — Soybean Yields
notes: Soy — yield_kg_ha: Source for global average of 2,800 kg/ha.

title: Our World in Data — Corn (Maize) Yields
notes: Corn — yield_kg_ha: Source for world average of ~5,600 kg/ha.

title: USDA ERS — Feed Grains Sector at a Glance
notes: Corn — pesticide_kg_ha and fertilizer_kg_ha: Used for US corn agricultural statistics including pesticide and fertilizer application rates.

title: UC Davis — Alfalfa for Livestock
notes: Alfalfa — yield_kg_ha: Source for typical yield and agronomic characteristics.
Alfalfa — water_per_kg: Source for water use as livestock feed.
Alfalfa — human_food flag: Confirmed primarily a livestock feed crop (human_food = 0).

title: FAO — Alfalfa Crop Information
notes: Alfalfa — production statistics: FAO reference for alfalfa yield, water use, and agricultural characteristics.

title: USDA National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS)
notes: Grass hay — yield_kg_ha: Source for US hay yield and acreage data.
Sorghum — yield_kg_ha: Source for US sorghum acreage and yield data.

title: AgMRC — Sorghum
notes: Sorghum — yield_kg_ha: Agricultural Marketing Resource Center profile for sorghum yield and production statistics.

title: Wikipedia — List of Animals by Number of Neurons
notes: Beef cattle — neuron_count: ~3 billion neurons, based on Herculano-Houzel isotropic fractionator methodology.
Broiler chicken and layer hen — neuron_count: ~221 million neurons in domestic chicken.
Pig — neuron_count: ~2.23 billion neurons.
Tuna — neuron_count: ~30 million neurons.

title: UNL BeefWatch — Pounds of Meat from a Beef Animal
notes: Beef cattle — weight_kg: Source for typical slaughter weight.
Beef cattle — dressing percentage: 63%, used to convert live-weight FCR to kg feed per kg dressed meat.

title: Frontiers in Neuroanatomy — Chicken Neuron Count
notes: Chicken — neuron_count: Peer-reviewed source confirming 221 million neurons in domestic chicken (Red Junglefowl).

title: UMaine Extension — Understanding Poultry Yields
notes: Broiler chicken — dressing percentage: 72–75%, used to convert live-weight FCR to kg feed per kg dressed meat.

title: Feedlot Magazine — What's In A Feedlot Diet
notes: Beef cattle — feed composition: Source for feedlot finishing ration breakdown including corn, soy, alfalfa, hay, and sorghum percentages.

title: Wikipedia — Feed Conversion Ratio
notes: Beef cattle — FCR: ~6.0 kg feed per kg live weight gain for feedlot cattle.
Broiler chicken — FCR: ~1.6 kg feed per kg live weight gain for modern commercial broilers.
General: Reference for FCR definitions and species-level values.

title: Penn State Extension — Beef Carcass Yields and Losses
notes: Beef cattle — dressing percentage: 63%, used to convert FCR to kg feed per kg dressed beef.

title: Farm Progress — Alfalfa in Finishing Rations
notes: Beef cattle — feed composition: Confirmed alfalfa inclusion at ~10–12% of feedlot finishing ration.

title: Sorghum Checkoff — Beef Feeding Guide
notes: Beef cattle — feed composition: Source for sorghum inclusion rate (~5%) in beef cattle finishing diets.

title: Feed Strategy — Traditional US Broiler Feed Formulations
notes: Broiler chicken — feed composition: Corn 55–65%, soy 20–28%, sorghum 3–6%, varying by growth stage.

title: PMC — Atypical Feed Ingredients for Broiler Chickens
notes: Broiler chicken — feed composition: Peer-reviewed confirmation of typical sorghum inclusion rates in commercial broiler diets.

title: Our World in Data — Tomato Yields
notes: Tomato — yield_kg_ha: Global average of 38,000 kg/ha based on FAO data.

title: Our World in Data — Rice Yields
notes: Rice — yield_kg_ha: Global average paddy rice yield of 4,700 kg/ha based on FAO data.

title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
notes: General: Comprehensive peer-reviewed global crop water footprint database; primary source for most water_per_kg values.
Almonds — water_per_kg: 13,080 L/kg; averaged with ScienceDirect California study (source 7, 10,240 L/kg) → final 11,660 L/kg.
Walnuts — water_per_kg: 7,744 L/kg; averaged with ScienceDirect tree nuts study (source 34, 9,279 L/kg) → final 8,512 L/kg.
Pistachios — water_per_kg: 10,697 L/kg; averaged with ScienceDirect tree nuts study (source 34, 11,356 L/kg) → final 11,027 L/kg.
Hazelnuts — water_per_kg: 9,807 L/kg; averaged with ScienceDirect tree nuts study (source 34, 10,511 L/kg) → final 10,159 L/kg.
Peanuts — water_per_kg: 3,740 L/kg; averaged with secondary sources → final 3,537 L/kg.

title: Pig333 — Feed Conversion Ratio in Pigs
notes: Pig — FCR: ~2.8 kg feed per kg live weight gain.
Pig — dressing percentage: ~73%, used to calculate kg feed per kg dressed pork.

title: Seafish — Atlantic Salmon Feed Profile
notes: Farmed salmon — FCR: ~1.15 kg feed per kg live weight gain.
Farmed salmon — dressing percentage: ~90%, used to calculate kg feed per kg dressed fillet.
Farmed salmon — feed composition: Soy ~19%, wheat ~10% of diet by weight.

notes: Cashews — water_per_kg: 45,914 L/kg (used directly; no conflicting peer-reviewed source found).
Walnuts — water_per_kg: 9,279 L/kg; averaged with Mekonnen & Hoekstra (source 30, 7,744 L/kg) → final 8,512 L/kg.
Pistachios — water_per_kg: 11,356 L/kg; averaged with Mekonnen & Hoekstra (source 30, 10,697 L/kg) → final 11,027 L/kg.

title: Champrix — Feed Conversion Ratio in Layer Hens
notes: Layer hen — FCR (productive period only): ~2.2 kg feed per kg eggs during the laying phase.
Layer hen — FCR note: This is the laying-phase baseline only; see source 38 for the full lifetime calculation that also includes chick and pullet phases.

title: Alberta Milk — Feed Required per Litre of Milk
notes: Dairy cow — feed ratio (productive period only): ~1.35 kg feed per kg milk during active lactation.
Dairy cow — feed note: This is the lactation-phase baseline only; see source 39 for the full lifetime calculation that also includes dry periods and heifer rearing.

title: The Poultry Site — Layer Hen Feed and Production Over Lifetime
notes: Layer hen — lifetime feed: ~2 kg chick phase + ~9 kg pullet rearing + ~58 kg laying phase = ~69 kg total feed per bird.
Layer hen — lifetime egg output: ~300 eggs ≈ 17.1 kg eggs per bird over productive life.
Layer hen — lifetime FCR: 69 ÷ 17.1 ≈ 4.04 kg feed per kg eggs.
Layer hen — feed split applied: 60% corn / 20% soy → corn 2.42 kg, soy 0.81 kg per kg eggs.

title: Dairy Global — Feed Efficiency in Dairy Cows
notes: Dairy cow — lifetime feed breakdown: ~36,450 kg lactation feed (1.35 kg/kg milk × 27,000 kg milk) + ~2,700 kg dry-period feed (3 periods × 60 days × 15 kg/day) + ~5,840 kg heifer rearing (730 days × 8 kg DM/day) = ~44,990 kg total.
Dairy cow — lifetime milk output: ~27,000 kg per cow over productive life.
Dairy cow — lifetime FCR: 44,990 ÷ 27,000 ≈ 1.67 kg feed per kg milk.
Dairy cow — feed split applied: 55% corn / 25% alfalfa / 10% soy / 7% grass hay → corn 0.92, alfalfa 0.42, soy 0.17, hay 0.12 kg per kg milk.

title: EA Forum — Neuron Count Measures May Underweight Suffering in Farmed Fish
notes: Atlantic salmon — neuron_count: value used is 130,000,000 (130 million), derived as follows.

The commonly cited figure of 10 million neurons for salmon originates from an empirical count of a 0.75g zebrafish. Applying this to a ~3,000g Atlantic salmon is biologically indefensible — the figure does not scale with body or brain size.

Bruns & Davies (2022) ran a Monte Carlo simulation estimating 8–64 million neurons for salmon-sized fish, but this range was derived from data on fish under 4g and extrapolated across a >750× weight gap, so the uncertainty is substantial.

A scaling-adjusted estimate: applying Invincible Wellbeing's vertebrate curve-fitting formula to a 3,000g salmon yields ~1.9 billion neurons, but fish brains are roughly 1/15th the relative size of mammals and birds at equivalent body weight. Dividing by 15 gives ~130 million, which is the value used here.

All estimates carry significant uncertainty. The 130M figure is the most biologically grounded available estimate given the absence of direct neurological counts for Atlantic salmon at commercial harvest size.
